home.bio: I'm a full stack web developer based in Gilwe (Geluwe), I like making "cool stuff" and discovering new things.

about.title: About me
about.subtitle: My story
about.teachingText: In 2017 I started as instructor in equestrian sports. Since then I learned a lot about teaching and how to work with people. I currently work as an instructor at {link}.
about.educationText1: In 2018 I started the graduate education in Multimedia & Information Technologies. There I learned all the basics of programming and web development.
about.educationText2: After MIT I started the bachelors degree in Multimedia & Communication Technologies as {link}.
about.experienceText1: In February 2023 I started as an intern at {link1}. After my internship I started working there as a {role}.
about.experienceText2: I transitioned to become a {role} at {link2}. Excited to bring my experience to new projects!

azure.title: Netflix
azure.aboutText: This project was developed as part of my bachelor thesis, exploring the integration of Search as a Service with unstructured data. Built with Vue.js and TailwindCSS, it demonstrates how Azure Cognitive Search and Amazon OpenSearch can index and query a large unstructured dataset — a text file containing over 8,000 movies and TV shows — delivering fast, relevant search results through a Netflix-style browsing experience.

vital.title: Vital Cities
vital.aboutText: Vital Cities is a group project I developed during my studies, in collaboration with classmates. The assignment was to design and build an engaging public-facing website for Vital Cities — an organization dedicated to helping cities become more sustainable and livable. We focused on crafting a highly interactive experience with rich animations, smooth page transitions, and a clean, modern aesthetic to effectively communicate their mission and inspire civic engagement.

binance.title: Binance
binance.aboutText: This concept project was designed and prototyped entirely in Adobe XD during my studies. The idea was to create a companion smartwatch app for cryptocurrency enthusiasts, letting users monitor their portfolio, track real-time prices, and receive alerts — all from their wrist. I used Adobe AfterEffects to craft fluid, polished animations and integrated them into the prototype with LottieFiles for a seamless, interactive demo experience.

bikeRental.title: Bike Rental
bikeRental.aboutText: This full-stack Progressive Web App was a school project built together with Achille Dupont. It enables users to rent bikes, view real-time bike locations on an interactive map, and manage their rentals through a mobile-first interface. On the backend, an admin panel lets operators add and remove bikes, track rental statuses, and monitor the entire fleet. The app combines Vue.js on the frontend with a NestJS and GraphQL API on the backend.

error.title: Page not found
error.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

chat.welcome: Hey! I'm Robbe's virtual assistant. Ask me about his skills, projects, experience, or how to get in touch!
chat.greeting: Hi there! How can I help you? You can ask about my projects, skills, experience, or how to reach me.
chat.skills: Robbe works with Vue.js, TypeScript, React, Drupal, PHP, NestJS, GraphQL, TailwindCSS, Node.js, and more. He's a full-stack developer who enjoys building modern web applications.
chat.projects: Robbe has built several projects: a Netflix-style search app using Azure Cognitive Search, a Vital Cities interactive website with Gatsby, a crypto smartwatch prototype in Adobe XD, and a full-stack BikeRental PWA with Vue and NestJS. You can check them out on the Projects page!
chat.contact: You can reach Robbe via email at robbe.saelens@telenet.be or by phone at 0471 95 81 95. He's also on LinkedIn, GitHub, Instagram, and Facebook — links are on the home page!
chat.experience: Robbe currently works at Dynamate as a Drupal backend developer. He previously worked at Your Mind Our Work as a PHP & Magento developer. Before that, he's been teaching equestrian sports since 2017.
chat.education: Robbe holds a Bachelor's degree in Multimedia & Communication Technologies (Next Web Developer) from Howest. He also completed a graduate degree in Multimedia & Information Technologies.
chat.about: Robbe Saelens is a full-stack web developer based in Geluwe, Belgium. He loves building cool stuff and discovering new technologies. He currently works at Dynamate and has a background in both development and teaching.
chat.thanks: You're welcome! Let me know if you have any other questions.
chat.bye: Bye! Feel free to reach out anytime. Have a great day!
chat.help: Available topics: skills, projects, experience, education, contact, about. Just type what you'd like to know!
chat.fallback: I'm not sure about that. Try asking about my skills, projects, experience, education, or how to contact me!
